revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Hoshi-jinja (Nagoya)]][[ja:星神社 (名古屋市)]] {{Expand Japanese|星神社 (名古屋市)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Hoshi Shrine | native_name = 星神社 (名古屋市) | image = Hoshi-Jinja.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Hoshi Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|35.214793|136.881209}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | website = {{Official website|https://www.hoshijinjya.com/}} }} {{nihongo|'''Hoshi Shrine'''|星神社 (名古屋市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Ronsha|lt=Shikinai Ronsha|ja|論社|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11465292|ltq=Q11465292|qid=Q11465292|1=List of Shikinaisha in Owari Province}} ** Series ordinal: 99 ** P155: {{ill|Q135039541|ltq=Q135039541|qid=Q135039541|1=Oi Shrine (Q135039541)}} ** P156: {{ill|Wohariheno Shrine|lt=Wohariheno Shrine|qid=Q135039543|1=Wohariheno Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q1207973|ltq=Q1207973|qid=Q1207973|1=Nishi-ku, Nagoya}} * {{ill|Q514506|ltq=Q514506|qid=Q514506|1=Owari Province}} * {{ill|Yamada district, Owari|lt=Yamada district, Owari|ja|山田郡 (尾張国)|zh|山田郡 (尾張國)|qid=Q11470699|1=Yamada district, Owari}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|35.214793|136.881209}} == official website (P856) == * https://www.hoshijinjya.com/ * https://goen-hoshijinjya.com/ == image (P18) == * Hoshi-Jinja.jpg == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181439 ** P3831: ᥓᥣᥱ ᥙᥣᥢᥱ|tet|Japaun|te|జపాన్|tg|Ҷопон|th|ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|thnews|หมวดหมู่:ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|tig|ጃፓን|ti|ጃፓን|tk|Ýaponiýa|tl|Hapon|tly|Japonijə|tn|Japan|tok|ma Nijon|to|Siapani|tpi|Siapan|trv|Tanah Tunux|tr|Japonya|trnews|Kategori:Japonya|trquote|Japonya|trvoyage|Japonya|ts|Japani|tt|Япония|tum|Japan|tw|Japan|tyv|Япония|ty|Tāpōnē|udm|Япония|ug|ياپونىيە|uk|Японія|uknews|Категорія:Японія|ukquote|Японія|ukvoyage|Японія|ur|جاپان|uz|Yaponiya|vec|Japon|vep|Japonii|ve|Japan|vi|Nhật Bản|vibooks|Các quốc gia/Nhật Bản|vivoyage|Nhật Bản|vls|Japan|vo|Yapän|war|Hapon|wa|Djapon|wo|Sapoŋ|wuu|日本|xal|Ниxуудин Нутг|xh|IJapani|xmf|იაპონია|yi|יאפאן|yo|Japan|za|Nyiedbonj|zea|Japan|zgh|ⵏⵉⴱⵓⵏ|zh_classical|日本|zh_min_nan|Ji̍t-pún|zh_yue|日本|zh|日本|zhnews|Category:日本|zhquote|日本|zhvoyage|日本|zu|IJaphani|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 7180005001093 == Commons category (P373) == * Hoshi-jinja (Nagoya) == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Sakanihano Shrine|lt=Sakanihano Shrine|qid=Q135039542|1=Sakanihano Shrine}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '愛知県名古屋市西区上小田井1-172', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Hoshi-jinja (Nagoya)}} [[JA:星神社 (名古屋市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11512981}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |画像 = [[画像:Hoshi-Jinja.jpg|270px]]
Torii and haiden |名称 = Hoshi Shrine |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|qid=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|qid=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Nishi Ward (Nagoya)|Q1207973|qid=Q1207973|lt=Nishi Ward|1=Nishi-ku, Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Kamiotai|Q11358809|qid=Q11358809|1=Kamiotai}}1-172 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = Four deities including Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} candidate shrine
former designated {{ill|lt=Village Shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory, p.22
11th grade shrine |創建 = Unknown (possibly before the {{ill|lt=13th century|Q7049|qid=Q7049|1=13th century}}) |別名 = Hoshinomiya
Hoshi no Yashiro |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭 = Second Monday of October |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Hoshi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{Location map|Nagoya|label=Hoshi Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} [[file:Hoshi Jinja 20121201.JPG|thumb|right|220px|Stone monument]] [[file:Hoshi Jinja Mukunoki 20130512.JPG|thumb|right|220px|Muku tree (May 2013)]] '''Hoshi Shrine''' (ほしじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kamiotai|Q11358809|qid=Q11358809|1=Kamiotai}}, {{ill|lt=Nishi Ward (Nagoya)|Q1207973|qid=Q1207973|lt=Nishi Ward|1=Nishi-ku, Nagoya}}, {{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|qid=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|qid=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is considered a candidate shrine for "Sakaniwa Shrine" of Yamada District, {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|qid=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. === History === The founding date is unknown. Shrine tradition says it was established in the Ninna era by descendants of {{ill|lt=Ōe no Otodo|Q11436894|qid=Q11436894|1=Ōe no Otondo}} living near this area. The oldest surviving {{ill|lt=munafuda|Q8008834|qid=Q8008834|1=Munafuda}} is from {{ill|lt=Kōan|Q389180|qid=Q389180|1=Kōan (Kamakura period)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1280|Q5536|qid=Q5536|1=1280}}). ''{{ill|lt=Owari-shi|Q137855962|qid=Q137855962|1=Owarishi}}'' records that there was also a munafuda from {{ill|lt=Tokuji|Q1327170|qid=Q1327170|1=Tokuji}} 2 ({{ill|lt=1307|Q5717|qid=Q5717|1=1307}}), and one stating construction in the 12th month of {{ill|lt=Ryakuō|Q1146182|qid=Q1146182|1=Ryakuō}} 4 ({{ill|lt=1341|Q6062|qid=Q6062|1=1341}}) by Ukonshō Fujiwara Saneki{{Efn|Possibly {{ill|lt=Ichijō Saneki|Q11352803|qid=Q11352803|1=Ichijō Saneaki}}, but uncertain.}}. War fires burned the shrine buildings along with treasures, sacred objects, and writings about shrine origins. It is said to have once had shrine lands of 4 chō 8 tan, but these were confiscated in the time of {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Q187550|qid=Q187550|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}}. One theory says the former site was around present {{ill|lt=Sakaido-chō|Q38277934|qid=Q38277934|1=Sakaido-chō}}. In {{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|qid=Q7753|1=1872}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5), it was ranked as a village shrine; in {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|qid=Q2048|1=1907}} (Meiji 40), it became a designated village shrine. Since the Edo period, the shrine side has claimed it is the Yamada District "Sakaniwa Shrine" listed in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. ''Owari-shi'' cites as evidence a Genroku-era report by shrine official Aoki Shinano-no-kami that wrote "Sakaniwa Hoshinomiya"{{Sfn|Nagoya Local Culture Society|1987|p=1}}. It also mentions an old secret text from the Kanpyō era written "Sakaniwa Hoshi no Miya"{{Sfn|Nagoya Local Culture Society|1987|p=1}}, though its reliability is unclear. Yamada District's "Sakaniwa Shrine" was identified by {{ill|lt=Amano Nobukage|Q6535030|qid=Q6535030|1=Amano Sadakage}} with Sannō Myōjin of Sakaba village (the present {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sakaniwa Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} in {{ill|lt=Komaki|Q389537|qid=Q389537|1=Komaki}}), and that became standard theory{{Efn|Because the place name Sakaba resembles Sakaniwa, the shrine called Sannō Myōjin in Sakaba village was identified as Sakaniwa Shrine. The current Sakaniwa Shrine in Komaki was renamed from Sannō Myōjin based on this theory{{Sfn|Nagoya Local Culture Society|1987|p=2}}.}}. However, Komaki clearly lay in {{ill|lt=Kasugai District|Q11513576|qid=Q11513576|1=Kasugai district}} rather than Yamada District, so it is not considered a strong candidate for the original "Sakaniwa Shrine." === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Ōkuninushi|Q276944|qid=Q276944|lt=Ōanamuji-no-Mikoto|1=Ōkuninushi}} * {{ill|lt=Amatsu-Mikaboshi|Q4740636|qid=Q4740636|lt=Ame-no-Kagase-o|1=Amatsu-Mikaboshi}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Cowherd Star|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Vega|1=UNKNOWN}} === Precinct shrines === * {{ill|lt=Tenmangū|Q798838|qid=Q798838|lt=Tenjin-sha|1=Tenmangū}} * {{ill|lt=Tsushima Shrine|Q705136|qid=Q705136|lt=Tsushima-sha|1=Tsushima Shrine}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shinmei-sha|1=UNKNOWN}} ** Kōnomiya Owari Ōkunitama Shrine * {{ill|lt=Konpira Shrine|UNKNOWN|lt=Kotohira-sha}} * {{ill|lt=Akiba Shrine|Q11595841|qid=Q11595841|lt=Akiba-sha|1=Akiba Shrines}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Inari-sha|1=UNKNOWN}} === Festivals === * Last Sunday of March - Hatsuuma Grand Festival * July 15 - Tennō Festival * August 7 - Tanabata Festival * Second Monday of October - Annual festival === Access === * {{ill|lt=Nagoya Railroad|Q30850|qid=Q30850|lt=Meitetsu|1=Meitetsu}}{{ill|lt=Inuyama Line|Q6810411|qid=Q6810411|lt=Inuyama Line|1=Meitetsu Inuyama Line}} - about 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Nakaotai Station|Q11363923|qid=Q11363923|1=Naka-Otai Station}}. * {{ill|lt=Nagoya Municipal Subway|Q1144874|qid=Q1144874|1=Nagoya Municipal Subway}}{{ill|lt=Tsurumai Line|Q1043806|qid=Q1043806|lt=Tsurumai Line|1=Tsurumai Line}} - about 10 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Shōnai Ryokuchi Kōen Station|Q862911|qid=Q862911|1=Shōnai Ryokuchi Kōen Station}}. === Other === The three {{ill|lt=muku trees|Q4779482|qid=Q4779482|1=Aphananthe aspera}} near the front entrance were designated on February 27, {{ill|lt=1995|Q2068|qid=Q2068|1=1995}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|qid=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 7) as Nagoya's {{ill|lt=Important Urban Landscape Buildings, etc.|Q11415135|qid=Q11415135|1=Important Urban Landscape Buildings, etc.}} (urban landscape preservation trees){{Cite news|url=https://www.city.nagoya.jp/kankou/category/358-1-0-0-0-0-0-0-0-0.html|title=Nagoya City: designated important urban landscape buildings etc.|publisher=Nagoya City|work=Housing and Urban Development Bureau, Cityscape Office|date=2012-09-25|accessdate=2012-11-15}}. The largest is about 18 m tall with trunk circumference over 4 m. At the honden completion dedication ceremony held on the night of {{ill|lt=November 12|Q2986|qid=Q2986|1=November 12}}, {{ill|lt=2019|Q25274|qid=Q25274|1=2019}} ({{ill|lt=Reiwa|Q62609017|qid=Q62609017|1=Reiwa era}} 1), {{ill|lt=Mieko Hirota|Q3543602|qid=Q3543602|1=Mieko Hirota}} offered a song rendition of "When You Wish Upon a Star"; to commemorate this, a stone monument noting it was placed in the precincts. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === Sources === {{Reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|和書|title=Aichi Prefecture Shrine Directory|publisher=Aichi Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines|year=1992|id={{全国書誌番号|93018567}}}} === Related items === * {{ill|lt=Tanabata|Q13423122|qid=Q13423122|1=Qixi Festival}} * {{ill|lt=Tanabata Shrine|Q11430658|qid=Q11430658|1=Tanabata Shrine (Q11430658)}} - another shrine deeply connected to Tanabata. * {{ill|lt=List of shrines in Aichi Prefecture|Q30925254|qid=Q30925254|1=list of Shinto shrines in Aichi Prefecture}} === External links === {{osm box|n|5066342803}} * {{official}} * [http://toppy.net/nagoya/nishi12.html Walking Nagoya "Otai": Is a man's only move to push hard?] * {{Commonscat-inline}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Hoshi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Hoshi Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagoya]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:History of Nishi ward]] [[Category:Son-sha in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:Important buildings and areas in Nagoya's urban landscape]] [[Category:Buildings in Nishi ward]] [[Category:Tanabata]]
